[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: What a funeral in Spain tells us about motorcycling in the United States - RevZilla
[METADATA/INTRO]: When I posted the news last month of the death of Angel Nieto, I didn't expect a big reaction from our U.S.-centric readership, even though it was top news in Spain and notable throughout Europe. He was legendary, but from...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Just look at this video of a portion of the thousands of riders who followed the car carrying Nieto's remains to his final mass. What struck me about this video was the diversity of the machines. Sport bikes. Naked bikes, large and small. Luxury tourers and sport-tourers. Adventure bikes and dual-sports. Japanese bikes and European bikes and Harley-Davidsons. Scooters, of course. Even three-wheeled scooters. In other words, just about everything that could be considered a motorcycle. 
 What that means is that a guy who was famous for racing motorcycles, and for helping future generations of racers advance in their careers, drew a full cross section of motorcyclists to come out for the day and say farewell. The EFE news agency reported that about 45,000 motorcyclists took place in the caravana to see off Nieto.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Sept. 11, 2021
[TITLE]: St. Louis shooting linked to motorcycle rally, few issues elsewhere
[METADATA/INTRO]: Bikers take over Interstate 270 south in Creve Coeur during the Ride of the Century on Saturday, Sept. 11, 2021. Every year thousands of bikers come to St. Louis to do rides and perform stunts all over the metro area...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Bikers take over Interstate 270 south in Creve Coeur during the Ride of the Century on Saturday, Sept. 11, 2021. Every year thousands of bikers come to St. Louis to do rides and perform stunts all over the metro area as part of the event. Photo by Daniel Shular, dshular@post-dispatch.com 
 A biker rides a wheelie with their leg over the front wheel on Interstate 270 south in Creve Coeur during the Ride of the Century on Saturday, Sept. 11, 2021. Every year thousands of bikers come to St. Louis to do rides and perform stunts all over the metro area. Photo by Daniel Shular, dshular@post-dispatch.com
